note: The LORD who shepherds is the Word who was with God.
The shepherd's provision and the Father who feeds the birds.
"For thou art with me" / "for I am with thee" — the presence formula.
David named among the heroes of faith.
The battle is the LORD's; be still and know.
"In the beginning" — John deliberately reopens Genesis.
Creation light and the light of men.
Evil meant for harm, worked by God for good.
God's purposeful plans for his people.
Jesus' teaching on anxiety echoed by Paul (same Greek verb, merimnao).
Peace beyond understanding; peace not as the world gives.
Shalom promised, peace guarding the heart.
He shall direct thy paths / he leadeth me in paths of righteousness.
Faith's evidence of things unseen; persuasion nothing can shake.
Rest for souls; lying down in green pastures.
The love that gave the Son is the love nothing can sever.
The greatest of these is love.
